summary: Set 083 S2: shipped the verification-integrity close gate, both layers deterministic. Layer 1 (vocabulary): disposition verification_method legal set is now (api, manual-via-other-engine, skipped); the incident's bare 'manual' and the Set 026 'queue' are rejected with naming messages, in validate_disposition, the JSON schema (parity, L-066-1), and at close time; the vocabulary sub-check (check_verification_method_vocabulary) runs on EVERY close path. Layer 2 (evidence): a claimed non-null verdict (explicit or api-status-derived) must be corroborated - api requires a session-verification metrics row for this (set, session) whose verifier provider, resolved via the model registry only (row provider strings untrusted; unresolvable identity fails closed), differs from the session-state orchestrator provider, plus an sN-verification*.md artifact; manual-via-other-engine/skipped require budget.yaml's zero-budget declaration with a matching method. Null-verdict SKIP closes (Set 080 S1 shape) stay legal. --manual-verify bypasses the evidence layer only (attested, logged, passed-with-note row); --force runs the check and now records the disposition's legal method honestly instead of hard-coding 'skipped'. Hard-block in both interactive and headless modes; every refusal prints the exact venv verify_session invocation. 47-test matrix incl. the live incident as an end-to-end regression fixture; 9 existing fixture files seeded with real evidence. Dogfood: all four verification rounds ran through the S1 verify_session CLI; gpt-5-4 found one new real Major per round across rounds 1-3 (metrics-row provider not registry-resolved; --manual-verify laundering the illegal token; --force misrecording verification.method), all fixed with regression tests; round 4 VERIFIED. Suite 2524 -> 2571 passed, 5 baseline skips.

reason.specifics: Routed analysis (gemini-pro, Step 8): the high-risk dual-release component of Session 3 dictates the choice - S2 calibration and L-078-1 show less capable models introduce release-blocking, registry-blind errors on procedural release work; gpt-5.4 @ medium is the single-orchestrator form (a split option - claude-fable-5 for the wide-shallow doc/template tasks, gpt-5.4 @ high for UAT/critique/releases - was also offered for the operator to choose). Supersedes the pre-set claude-sonnet-4-6 recommendation, which predated the S1/S2 calibration data.
